title: -Authorization to Accept One-Time $10,000 Stipend for Service on the California Local Government Agency Executive Committee

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager or designee to accept a $10,000
stipend award, for Fiscal Year 2026, from Santa Cruz County Health Services Agency.
This funding will support the City of Berkeley’s Health, Housing, and Community
Services Department’s Medi-Cal Administrative Activities Program.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The City is eligible to receive a one-time $10,000 stipend as a result of the Local
Government Agency (“LGA”) Coordinator’s service on the California Local Government
Agency Executive Committee. The stipend will be deposited into Fund 313 (Targeted
Case Management). The LGA Coordinator is responsible for oversight of the City’s
Medi-Cal Administrative Activities programs.
sections.background:
The Medi-Cal Administrative Activities (“MAA”) program, established on January 1,
1995, pursuant to Welfare and Institutions (“W&I”) Code § 14132.47, allows participating
Local Governmental Agencies (“LGAs”) to receive federal reimbursement for
administrative activities that directly support efforts to identify and enroll potentially
eligible individuals into Medi-Cal.
MAA activities aim to promote healthcare access within the county public health system,
reduce long-term health care costs for at-risk populations, and coordinate client care
across various health providers. Eligible activities include, but are not limited to,
conducting Medi-Cal outreach, facilitating eligibility determinations, participating in Medi-
Cal program planning, and contract management.
The City of Berkeley has actively participated in the MAA program for over a decade,
allowing the City to claim federal reimbursement for administrative activities that support
Medi-Cal outreach, enrollment, and coordination. These reimbursements help offset the
cost of services staff are already providing—such as referrals, care coordination, and

In Fiscal Year 2024–2025, Quarter 4, the City expanded its participation in the MAA
program by adding Neighborhood Services in the City Manager’s Office as a new
claiming unit. This expansion will further maximize eligible reimbursements and
reinforce the City's commitment to resource efficiency.
Additionally, the City’s LGA Coordinator was selected to serve as a Member-at-Large
on the California LGA Executive Committee, which brought a $10,000
sections.rationale:
Accepting this funding will allow the department to continue supporting the MAA
program and sustain activities that generate federal reimbursement for critical Medi-Cal
covered services.
sections.alternatives:
No alternative actions were identified.
CONTACT PERSON
Tamiko Huey, Senior Management Analyst, HHCS, (510) 725-2243
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
ACCEPTANCE OF STIPEND FOR SERVICE ON CALIFORNIA LOCAL GOVERNMENT
AGENCY EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley has participated in the Medi-Cal Administrative Activities
(“MAA”) program for over a decade, enabling the City to claim federal reimbursement for
administrative activities that support Medi-Cal outreach, enrollment, and coordination;
and
WHEREAS, the MAA program supports the City's goal to provide an efficient and
financially healthy government by leveraging federal resources to sustain essential public
health and human services; and
WHEREAS, the City’s designated MAA Coordinator was selected to serve as a Member-
at-Large on the California Local Government Agency Executive Committee, for Fiscal
Year 2025–2026; and
WHEREAS, this statewide leadership service entitles the City to receive a one-time
$10,000 stipend.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager or designee is authorized to accept the one-time $10,000 stipend from the
County of Santa Cruz for the MAA Coordinator’s service on the California Local
Government Agency Executive Committee and deposit the stipend into Fund 313.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the accepted funds shall be allocated to support the
department’s ongoing participation in the MAA program.
